# solution.py
"""Team_Collaboration_Manager
================================

A thread-safe, in-memory backend for a team collaboration platform.

The system models the core collaboration domain:

* **Users**      - team members with roles (member / admin).
* **Projects**   - time-boxed bodies of work owned by a user.
* **Tasks**      - units of work inside a project, optionally assigned to a
                   user, with a status lifecycle and timing metadata.
* **Messages**   - threaded discussion attached to a project or a task, with
                   file attachments and editing/deletion controlled by author.
* **Performance**- per-user dashboards: completion rate, average completion
                   time, and peer ratings.
* **Reports**    - project-progress, team-performance and individual reports,
                   exportable as CSV or PDF.

Design notes
------------
* A single re-entrant lock (``RLock``) guards every mutation and is shared by
  all six sub-services so that cross-entity operations stay atomic and the
  store remains consistent under concurrent access.
* Identifiers are drawn from one global, monotonically increasing counter, so
  every entity id is unique across the whole system and starts at 1.
* Every accessor returns a deep copy of stored data; callers can never mutate
  the authoritative state by holding on to a returned object or collection.

The module depends on the Python standard library only.
"""

from __future__ import annotations

import copy
import csv
import io
import threading
from collections import defaultdict
from dataclasses import dataclass, field
from datetime import datetime
from enum import Enum
from typing import Callable, Dict, List, Optional


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Enumerations
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class UserRole(Enum):
    """Role a user holds within the platform."""

    MEMBER = "member"
    ADMIN = "admin"


class TaskStatus(Enum):
    """Lifecycle state of a task."""

    NOT_STARTED = "not_started"
    IN_PROGRESS = "in_progress"
    COMPLETED = "completed"


class ReportFormat(Enum):
    """Supported export formats for a report."""

    CSV = "csv"
    PDF = "pdf"


class ReportType(Enum):
    """Kind of report produced by the reporting service."""

    PROJECT_PROGRESS = "project_progress"
    TEAM_PERFORMANCE = "team_performance"
    INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE = "individual_performance"


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Exception hierarchy
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationError(Exception):
    """Base class for all errors raised by the manager."""


class ValidationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when input fails validation."""


class NotFoundError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a referenced entity does not exist."""


class AuthorizationError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an actor is not permitted to perform an operation."""


class DuplicateError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when a uniqueness constraint would be violated."""


class ConflictError(TeamCollaborationError):
    """Raised when an operation conflicts with existing state."""


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Data models
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
@dataclass
class Attachment:
    """A file attached to a message."""

    filename: str
    size: int
    uploaded_by: int
    uploaded_at: datetime
    content: bytes = b""
    id: Optional[int] = None


@dataclass
class User:
    """A registered team member."""

    id: int
    username: str
    email: str
    role: UserRole
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Project:
    """A time-boxed project owned by a user."""

    id: int
    name: str
    owner_id: int
    start_date: datetime
    end_date: datetime
    description: str
    created_at: datetime


@dataclass
class Task:
    """A unit of work within a project."""

    id: int
    project_id: int
    title: str
    assignee_id: Optional[int]
    deadline: Optional[datetime]
    status: TaskStatus
    created_at: datetime
    started_at: Optional[datetime] = None
    completed_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Message:
    """A threaded message attached to a project or a task."""

    id: int
    author_id: int
    body: str
    project_id: Optional[int]
    task_id: Optional[int]
    parent_id: Optional[int]
    created_at: datetime
    attachments: List[Attachment] = field(default_factory=list)
    edited_at: Optional[datetime] = None


@dataclass
class Rating:
    """A peer rating left for a user."""

    id: int
    rater_id: int
    ratee_id: int
    score: int
    feedback: str
    task_id: Optional[int]
    created_at: datetime


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Internal sub-services (share the manager's lock)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class _BaseService:
    """Base for every sub-service; exposes the shared lock."""

    def __init__(self, manager: "TeamCollaborationManager") -> None:
        self._manager = manager
        self._lock = manager._lock


class _UserStore(_BaseService):
    def __init__(self, manager: "TeamCollaborationManager") -> None:
        super().__init__(manager)
        self._items: Dict[int, User] = {}
        self._by_username: Dict[str, int] = {}
        self._by_email: Dict[str, int] = {}


class _ProjectStore(_BaseService):
    def __init__(self, manager: "TeamCollaborationManager") -> None:
        super().__init__(manager)
        self._items: Dict[int, Project] = {}


class _TaskStore(_BaseService):
    def __init__(self, manager: "TeamCollaborationManager") -> None:
        super().__init__(manager)
        self._items: Dict[int, Task] = {}
        self._by_project: Dict[int, set] = defaultdict(set)
        self._by_assignee: Dict[int, set] = defaultdict(set)


class _MessageStore(_BaseService):
    def __init__(self, manager: "TeamCollaborationManager") -> None:
        super().__init__(manager)
        self._items: Dict[int, Message] = {}
        self._by_project: Dict[int, set] = defaultdict(set)
        self._by_task: Dict[int, set] = defaultdict(set)


class _PerformanceService(_BaseService):
    def __init__(self, manager: "TeamCollaborationManager") -> None:
        super().__init__(manager)
        self._ratings: Dict[int, Rating] = {}
        self._by_ratee: Dict[int, set] = defaultdict(set)


class _ReportService(_BaseService):
    pass


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Manager
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
class TeamCollaborationManager:
    """Facade orchestrating users, projects, tasks, messaging and reporting."""

    def __init__(self, now: Optional[Callable[[], datetime]] = None) -> None:
        self._now: Callable[[], datetime] = now if now is not None else datetime.now
        self._lock = threading.RLock()
        self._id_counter = 0

        # Sub-services all share ``self._lock``.
        self.users = _UserStore(self)
        self.projects = _ProjectStore(self)
        self.tasks = _TaskStore(self)
        self.messages = _MessageStore(self)
        self.performance = _PerformanceService(self)
        self.reports = _ReportService(self)

    # -- internal helpers ---------------------------------------------------
    def _alloc_id(self) -> int:
        """Return the next globally-unique id. Caller holds ``self._lock``."""
        self._id_counter += 1
        return self._id_counter

    def _require_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        user = self.users._items.get(user_id)
        if user is None:
            raise NotFoundError("user {0} not found".format(user_id))
        return user

    def _require_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        project = self.projects._items.get(project_id)
        if project is None:
            raise NotFoundError("project {0} not found".format(project_id))
        return project

    def _require_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        task = self.tasks._items.get(task_id)
        if task is None:
            raise NotFoundError("task {0} not found".format(task_id))
        return task

    def _require_message(self, message_id: int) -> Message:
        message = self.messages._items.get(message_id)
        if message is None:
            raise NotFoundError("message {0} not found".format(message_id))
        return message

    @staticmethod
    def _is_blank(value) -> bool:
        return not isinstance(value, str) or not value.strip()

    # ==================================================================
    # User management
    # ==================================================================
    def register_user(
        self, username: str, email: str, role: UserRole = UserRole.MEMBER
    ) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            if self._is_blank(username):
                raise ValidationError("username is required")
            if self._is_blank(email) or "@" not in email:
                raise ValidationError("a valid email is required")
            if not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")
            if username in self.users._by_username:
                raise DuplicateError("username already in use")
            if email in self.users._by_email:
                raise DuplicateError("email already in use")

            user_id = self._alloc_id()
            user = User(
                id=user_id,
                username=username,
                email=email,
                role=role,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.users._items[user_id] = user
            self.users._by_username[username] = user_id
            self.users._by_email[email] = user_id
            return copy.deepcopy(user)

    def get_user(self, user_id: int) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            return copy.deepcopy(self._require_user(user_id))

    def list_users(self) -> List[User]:
        with self._lock:
            ordered = sorted(self.users._items.values(), key=lambda u: u.id)
            return [copy.deepcopy(u) for u in ordered]

    def update_user(
        self,
        user_id: int,
        username: Optional[str] = None,
        email: Optional[str] = None,
        role: Optional[UserRole] = None,
    ) -> User:
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)

            if username is not None:
                if self._is_blank(username):
                    raise ValidationError("username is required")
                holder = self.users._by_username.get(username)
                if holder is not None and holder != user_id:
                    raise DuplicateError("username already in use")
            if email is not None:
                if self._is_blank(email) or "@" not in email:
                    raise ValidationError("a valid email is required")
                holder = self.users._by_email.get(email)
                if holder is not None and holder != user_id:
                    raise DuplicateError("email already in use")
            if role is not None and not isinstance(role, UserRole):
                raise ValidationError("role must be a UserRole")

            if username is not None and username != user.username:
                self.users._by_username.pop(user.username, None)
                user.username = username
                self.users._by_username[username] = user_id
            if email is not None and email != user.email:
                self.users._by_email.pop(user.email, None)
                user.email = email
                self.users._by_email[email] = user_id
            if role is not None:
                user.role = role

            return copy.deepcopy(user)

    def delete_user(self, user_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            user = self._require_user(user_id)

            for project in self.projects._items.values():
                if project.owner_id == user_id:
                    raise ConflictError("user owns one or more projects")

            for task_id in self.tasks._by_assignee.get(user_id, set()):
                task = self.tasks._items.get(task_id)
                if task is not None and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    raise ConflictError("user has open tasks assigned")

            del self.users._items[user_id]
            self.users._by_username.pop(user.username, None)
            self.users._by_email.pop(user.email, None)
            self.tasks._by_assignee.pop(user_id, None)

    # ==================================================================
    # Project management
    # ==================================================================
    def create_project(
        self,
        name: str,
        start_date: datetime,
        end_date: datetime,
        owner_id: int,
        description: str = "",
    ) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(owner_id)
            if self._is_blank(name):
                raise ValidationError("project name is required")
            if end_date < start_date:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot be before start_date")

            project_id = self._alloc_id()
            project = Project(
                id=project_id,
                name=name,
                owner_id=owner_id,
                start_date=start_date,
                end_date=end_date,
                description=description,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.projects._items[project_id] = project
            return copy.deepcopy(project)

    def get_project(self, project_id: int) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            return copy.deepcopy(self._require_project(project_id))

    def list_projects(self) -> List[Project]:
        with self._lock:
            ordered = sorted(self.projects._items.values(), key=lambda p: p.id)
            return [copy.deepcopy(p) for p in ordered]

    def update_project(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        name: Optional[str] = None,
        start_date: Optional[datetime] = None,
        end_date: Optional[datetime] = None,
        description: Optional[str] = None,
    ) -> Project:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)

            if name is not None and self._is_blank(name):
                raise ValidationError("project name is required")

            effective_start = start_date if start_date is not None else project.start_date
            effective_end = end_date if end_date is not None else project.end_date
            if effective_end < effective_start:
                raise ValidationError("end_date cannot be before start_date")

            if name is not None:
                project.name = name
            if start_date is not None:
                project.start_date = start_date
            if end_date is not None:
                project.end_date = end_date
            if description is not None:
                project.description = description

            return copy.deepcopy(project)

    def delete_project(self, project_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)

            task_ids = list(self.tasks._by_project.get(project_id, set()))
            for task_id in task_ids:
                self._remove_task_internal(task_id)
            self.tasks._by_project.pop(project_id, None)

            message_ids = list(self.messages._by_project.get(project_id, set()))
            for message_id in message_ids:
                self._remove_message_internal(message_id)
            self.messages._by_project.pop(project_id, None)

            del self.projects._items[project_id]

    # ==================================================================
    # Task management
    # ==================================================================
    def create_task(
        self,
        project_id: int,
        title: str,
        assignee_id: Optional[int] = None,
        deadline: Optional[datetime] = None,
    ) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            project = self._require_project(project_id)
            if self._is_blank(title):
                raise ValidationError("task title is required")
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self._require_user(assignee_id)
            if deadline is not None and deadline < project.start_date:
                raise ValidationError("deadline cannot be before project start")

            task_id = self._alloc_id()
            task = Task(
                id=task_id,
                project_id=project_id,
                title=title,
                assignee_id=assignee_id,
                deadline=deadline,
                status=TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.tasks._items[task_id] = task
            self.tasks._by_project[project_id].add(task_id)
            if assignee_id is not None:
                self.tasks._by_assignee[assignee_id].add(task_id)
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def get_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            return copy.deepcopy(self._require_task(task_id))

    def list_tasks_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            task_ids = self.tasks._by_project.get(project_id, set())
            tasks = [self.tasks._items[t] for t in task_ids if t in self.tasks._items]
            tasks.sort(key=lambda t: t.id)
            return [copy.deepcopy(t) for t in tasks]

    def list_tasks_by_assignee(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            task_ids = self.tasks._by_assignee.get(user_id, set())
            tasks = [self.tasks._items[t] for t in task_ids if t in self.tasks._items]
            tasks.sort(key=lambda t: t.id)
            return [copy.deepcopy(t) for t in tasks]

    def assign_task(self, task_id: int, assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._require_user(assignee_id)
            self._set_assignee_internal(task, assignee_id)
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def reassign_task(self, task_id: int, assignee_id: int) -> Task:
        return self.assign_task(task_id, assignee_id)

    def unassign_task(self, task_id: int) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            self._set_assignee_internal(task, None)
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def set_deadline(self, task_id: int, deadline: Optional[datetime]) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            if deadline is not None:
                project = self._require_project(task.project_id)
                if deadline < project.start_date:
                    raise ValidationError("deadline cannot be before project start")
            task.deadline = deadline
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    def update_status(self, task_id: int, status: TaskStatus) -> Task:
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(status, TaskStatus):
                raise ValidationError("status must be a TaskStatus")
            task = self._require_task(task_id)
            now = self._now()

            if status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = None
            elif status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                if task.started_at is None:
                    task.started_at = now
                task.completed_at = now
            else:  # NOT_STARTED
                task.started_at = None
                task.completed_at = None

            task.status = status
            return copy.deepcopy(task)

    # -- task internal helpers ---------------------------------------------
    def _set_assignee_internal(self, task: Task, assignee_id: Optional[int]) -> None:
        if task.assignee_id is not None:
            bucket = self.tasks._by_assignee.get(task.assignee_id)
            if bucket is not None:
                bucket.discard(task.id)
        task.assignee_id = assignee_id
        if assignee_id is not None:
            self.tasks._by_assignee[assignee_id].add(task.id)

    def _remove_task_internal(self, task_id: int) -> None:
        task = self.tasks._items.pop(task_id, None)
        if task is None:
            return
        bucket = self.tasks._by_project.get(task.project_id)
        if bucket is not None:
            bucket.discard(task_id)
        if task.assignee_id is not None:
            assignee_bucket = self.tasks._by_assignee.get(task.assignee_id)
            if assignee_bucket is not None:
                assignee_bucket.discard(task_id)
        # Cascade messages attached to this task.
        for message_id in list(self.messages._by_task.get(task_id, set())):
            self._remove_message_internal(message_id)
        self.messages._by_task.pop(task_id, None)

    # ==================================================================
    # Messaging
    # ==================================================================
    def post_message(
        self,
        author_id: int,
        body: str,
        project_id: Optional[int] = None,
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
        attachments: Optional[List] = None,
        parent_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(author_id)

            if (project_id is None) == (task_id is None):
                raise ValidationError(
                    "exactly one of project_id or task_id is required"
                )
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)

            attachment_specs = attachments or []
            if self._is_blank(body) and not attachment_specs:
                raise ValidationError("message body or an attachment is required")

            if parent_id is not None:
                self._require_message(parent_id)

            message_id = self._alloc_id()
            now = self._now()
            built_attachments = [
                self._build_attachment(filename, content, author_id, now)
                for filename, content in attachment_specs
            ]
            message = Message(
                id=message_id,
                author_id=author_id,
                body=body,
                project_id=project_id,
                task_id=task_id,
                parent_id=parent_id,
                created_at=now,
                attachments=built_attachments,
            )
            self.messages._items[message_id] = message
            if project_id is not None:
                self.messages._by_project[project_id].add(message_id)
            if task_id is not None:
                self.messages._by_task[task_id].add(message_id)
            return copy.deepcopy(message)

    def edit_message(self, message_id: int, author_id: int, new_body: str) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != author_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may edit a message")
            if self._is_blank(new_body) and not message.attachments:
                raise ValidationError("message body or an attachment is required")
            message.body = new_body
            message.edited_at = self._now()
            return copy.deepcopy(message)

    def add_attachment(
        self, message_id: int, author_id: int, filename: str, content: bytes
    ) -> Message:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != author_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may attach a file")
            if self._is_blank(filename):
                raise ValidationError("filename is required")
            message.attachments.append(
                self._build_attachment(filename, content, author_id, self._now())
            )
            return copy.deepcopy(message)

    def list_messages_by_project(self, project_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            message_ids = self.messages._by_project.get(project_id, set())
            msgs = [
                self.messages._items[m]
                for m in message_ids
                if m in self.messages._items
            ]
            msgs.sort(key=lambda m: m.id)
            return [copy.deepcopy(m) for m in msgs]

    def list_messages_by_task(self, task_id: int) -> List[Message]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_task(task_id)
            message_ids = self.messages._by_task.get(task_id, set())
            msgs = [
                self.messages._items[m]
                for m in message_ids
                if m in self.messages._items
            ]
            msgs.sort(key=lambda m: m.id)
            return [copy.deepcopy(m) for m in msgs]

    def delete_message(self, message_id: int, author_id: int) -> None:
        with self._lock:
            message = self._require_message(message_id)
            if message.author_id != author_id:
                raise AuthorizationError("only the author may delete a message")
            self._remove_message_internal(message_id)

    # -- message internal helpers ------------------------------------------
    def _build_attachment(
        self, filename: str, content: bytes, uploaded_by: int, uploaded_at: datetime
    ) -> Attachment:
        if self._is_blank(filename):
            raise ValidationError("filename is required")
        data = content if content is not None else b""
        return Attachment(
            filename=filename,
            size=len(data),
            uploaded_by=uploaded_by,
            uploaded_at=uploaded_at,
            content=bytes(data),
            id=self._alloc_id(),
        )

    def _remove_message_internal(self, message_id: int) -> None:
        message = self.messages._items.pop(message_id, None)
        if message is None:
            return
        if message.project_id is not None:
            bucket = self.messages._by_project.get(message.project_id)
            if bucket is not None:
                bucket.discard(message_id)
        if message.task_id is not None:
            bucket = self.messages._by_task.get(message.task_id)
            if bucket is not None:
                bucket.discard(message_id)

    # ==================================================================
    # Performance dashboard
    # ==================================================================
    def _user_tasks(self, user_id: int) -> List[Task]:
        task_ids = self.tasks._by_assignee.get(user_id, set())
        return [self.tasks._items[t] for t in task_ids if t in self.tasks._items]

    @staticmethod
    def _completion_rate(tasks: List[Task]) -> float:
        if not tasks:
            return 0.0
        completed = sum(1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
        return completed / len(tasks)

    @staticmethod
    def _average_completion_time(tasks: List[Task]) -> Optional[float]:
        durations = [
            (t.completed_at - t.started_at).total_seconds()
            for t in tasks
            if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
            and t.started_at is not None
            and t.completed_at is not None
        ]
        if not durations:
            return None
        return sum(durations) / len(durations)

    def get_completion_rate(self, user_id: int) -> float:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            return self._completion_rate(self._user_tasks(user_id))

    def get_average_completion_time(self, user_id: int) -> Optional[float]:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            return self._average_completion_time(self._user_tasks(user_id))

    def submit_rating(
        self,
        rater_id: int,
        ratee_id: int,
        score: int,
        feedback: str = "",
        task_id: Optional[int] = None,
    ) -> Rating:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(rater_id)
            self._require_user(ratee_id)
            if rater_id == ratee_id:
                raise ConflictError("a user cannot rate themselves")
            if not isinstance(score, int) or isinstance(score, bool):
                raise ValidationError("score must be an integer between 1 and 5")
            if score < 1 or score > 5:
                raise ValidationError("score must be between 1 and 5")
            if task_id is not None:
                self._require_task(task_id)

            rating_id = self._alloc_id()
            rating = Rating(
                id=rating_id,
                rater_id=rater_id,
                ratee_id=ratee_id,
                score=score,
                feedback=feedback or "",
                task_id=task_id,
                created_at=self._now(),
            )
            self.performance._ratings[rating_id] = rating
            self.performance._by_ratee[ratee_id].add(rating_id)
            return copy.deepcopy(rating)

    def _ratee_ratings(self, user_id: int) -> List[Rating]:
        rating_ids = self.performance._by_ratee.get(user_id, set())
        ratings = [
            self.performance._ratings[r]
            for r in rating_ids
            if r in self.performance._ratings
        ]
        ratings.sort(key=lambda r: r.id)
        return ratings

    def get_ratings_summary(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            ratings = self._ratee_ratings(user_id)
            count = len(ratings)
            average = (sum(r.score for r in ratings) / count) if count else 0.0
            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "count": count,
                "average": average,
                "ratings": [copy.deepcopy(r) for r in ratings],
            }

    def get_user_dashboard(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._user_tasks(user_id)
            ratings = self._ratee_ratings(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            rating_average = (
                sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                if rating_count
                else 0.0
            )
            return {
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": len(tasks),
                "completed_tasks": sum(
                    1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                ),
                "in_progress_tasks": sum(
                    1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
                ),
                "not_started_tasks": sum(
                    1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
                ),
                "completion_rate": self._completion_rate(tasks),
                "average_completion_time": self._average_completion_time(tasks),
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": rating_average,
            }

    # ==================================================================
    # Reports
    # ==================================================================
    def project_progress_report(self, project_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_project(project_id)
            now = self._now()
            task_ids = self.tasks._by_project.get(project_id, set())
            tasks = [self.tasks._items[t] for t in task_ids if t in self.tasks._items]

            status_counts = {status.value: 0 for status in TaskStatus}
            overdue = 0
            per_assignee: Dict[int, dict] = {}
            completed = 0

            for task in tasks:
                status_counts[task.status.value] += 1
                if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                    completed += 1
                is_overdue = (
                    task.deadline is not None
                    and task.deadline < now
                    and task.status != TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                )
                if is_overdue:
                    overdue += 1
                if task.assignee_id is not None:
                    bucket = per_assignee.setdefault(
                        task.assignee_id,
                        {"total": 0, "completed": 0, "overdue": 0},
                    )
                    bucket["total"] += 1
                    if task.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED:
                        bucket["completed"] += 1
                    if is_overdue:
                        bucket["overdue"] += 1

            total = len(tasks)
            for bucket in per_assignee.values():
                bucket["completion_rate"] = (
                    bucket["completed"] / bucket["total"] if bucket["total"] else 0.0
                )

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.PROJECT_PROGRESS.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "total_tasks": total,
                "status_counts": status_counts,
                "completion_rate": (completed / total) if total else 0.0,
                "overdue_tasks": overdue,
                "per_assignee": per_assignee,
                "generated_at": now,
            }

    def team_performance_report(self, project_id: Optional[int] = None) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            if project_id is not None:
                self._require_project(project_id)
                scoped_ids = self.tasks._by_project.get(project_id, set())
                tasks_in_scope = [
                    self.tasks._items[t]
                    for t in scoped_ids
                    if t in self.tasks._items
                ]
                user_ids = sorted(
                    {t.assignee_id for t in tasks_in_scope if t.assignee_id is not None}
                )

                def tasks_for(uid: int) -> List[Task]:
                    return [t for t in tasks_in_scope if t.assignee_id == uid]

            else:
                user_ids = sorted(self.users._items.keys())

                def tasks_for(uid: int) -> List[Task]:
                    return self._user_tasks(uid)

            rows = []
            for uid in user_ids:
                user_tasks = tasks_for(uid)
                ratings = self._ratee_ratings(uid)
                rating_count = len(ratings)
                rows.append(
                    {
                        "user_id": uid,
                        "total_tasks": len(user_tasks),
                        "completed_tasks": sum(
                            1
                            for t in user_tasks
                            if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                        ),
                        "completion_rate": self._completion_rate(user_tasks),
                        "average_completion_time": self._average_completion_time(
                            user_tasks
                        ),
                        "rating_count": rating_count,
                        "rating_average": (
                            sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                            if rating_count
                            else 0.0
                        ),
                    }
                )

            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.TEAM_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "project_id": project_id,
                "rows": rows,
                "generated_at": self._now(),
            }

    def individual_performance_report(self, user_id: int) -> dict:
        with self._lock:
            self._require_user(user_id)
            tasks = self._user_tasks(user_id)
            tasks.sort(key=lambda t: t.id)
            ratings = self._ratee_ratings(user_id)
            rating_count = len(ratings)
            return {
                "report_type": ReportType.INDIVIDUAL_PERFORMANCE.value,
                "user_id": user_id,
                "total_tasks": len(tasks),
                "completed_tasks": sum(
                    1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.COMPLETED
                ),
                "in_progress_tasks": sum(
                    1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS
                ),
                "not_started_tasks": sum(
                    1 for t in tasks if t.status == TaskStatus.NOT_STARTED
                ),
                "completion_rate": self._completion_rate(tasks),
                "average_completion_time": self._average_completion_time(tasks),
                "rating_count": rating_count,
                "rating_average": (
                    sum(r.score for r in ratings) / rating_count
                    if rating_count
                    else 0.0
                ),
                "tasks": [copy.deepcopy(t) for t in tasks],
                "generated_at": self._now(),
            }

    def export_report(self, report: dict, report_format: ReportFormat):
        with self._lock:
            if not isinstance(report, dict):
                raise ValidationError("report must be a report dictionary")
            if not isinstance(report_format, ReportFormat):
                raise ValidationError("report_format must be a ReportFormat")

            if report_format == ReportFormat.CSV:
                return self._export_csv(report)
            return self._export_pdf(report)

    # -- export helpers -----------------------------------------------------
    @staticmethod
    def _scalar(value) -> str:
        if value is None:
            return ""
        if isinstance(value, Enum):
            return str(value.value)
        if isinstance(value, float):
            return repr(value)
        if isinstance(value, (dict, list, tuple, set)):
            return str(value)
        return str(value)

    def _export_csv(self, report: dict) -> str:
        buffer = io.StringIO()
        writer = csv.writer(buffer)
        rows = report.get("rows")
        if isinstance(rows, list):
            if rows and isinstance(rows[0], dict):
                headers = list(rows[0].keys())
            else:
                headers = ["value"]
            writer.writerow(headers)
            for row in rows:
                if isinstance(row, dict):
                    writer.writerow([self._scalar(row.get(h)) for h in headers])
                else:
                    writer.writerow([self._scalar(row)])
        else:
            writer.writerow(["field", "value"])
            for key, value in report.items():
                writer.writerow([key, self._scalar(value)])
        return buffer.getvalue()

    def _export_pdf(self, report: dict) -> bytes:
        lines = self._report_to_lines(report)
        return _build_pdf(lines)

    def _report_to_lines(self, report: dict) -> List[str]:
        lines = ["Team Collaboration Report"]
        report_type = report.get("report_type", "report")
        lines.append("Type: {0}".format(report_type))
        for key, value in report.items():
            if key == "report_type":
                continue
            if key == "rows" and isinstance(value, list):
                lines.append("rows:")
                for row in value:
                    if isinstance(row, dict):
                        lines.append(
                            "  "
                            + "; ".join(
                                "{0}={1}".format(k, self._scalar(v))
                                for k, v in row.items()
                            )
                        )
                    else:
                        lines.append("  {0}".format(self._scalar(row)))
            elif key == "tasks" and isinstance(value, list):
                lines.append("tasks: {0} item(s)".format(len(value)))
            else:
                lines.append("{0}: {1}".format(key, self._scalar(value)))
        return lines


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Minimal PDF writer (standard library only)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def _pdf_escape(text: str) -> str:
    return (
        text.replace("\\", "\\\\")
        .replace("(", "\\(")
        .replace(")", "\\)")
    )


def _build_pdf(lines: List[str]) -> bytes:
    """Build a minimal, well-formed single-page PDF document from text lines."""
    parts = ["BT", "/F1 11 Tf", "72 720 Td", "14 TL"]
    first = True
    for line in lines:
        safe = _pdf_escape(line)
        if first:
            parts.append("({0}) Tj".format(safe))
            first = False
        else:
            parts.append("T*")
            parts.append("({0}) Tj".format(safe))
    parts.append("ET")
    content = ("\n".join(parts)).encode("latin-1", "replace")

    objects = [
        b"<< /Type /Catalog /Pages 2 0 R >>",
        b"<< /Type /Pages /Kids [3 0 R] /Count 1 >>",
        b"<< /Type /Page /Parent 2 0 R /MediaBox [0 0 612 792] "
        b"/Resources << /Font << /F1 4 0 R >> >> /Contents 5 0 R >>",
        b"<< /Type /Font /Subtype /Type1 /BaseFont /Helvetica >>",
        b"<< /Length "
        + str(len(content)).encode("latin-1")
        + b" >>\nstream\n"
        + content
        + b"\nendstream",
    ]

    body = bytearray(b"%PDF-1.4\n%\xe2\xe3\xcf\xd3\n")
    offsets = []
    for index, obj in enumerate(objects, start=1):
        offsets.append(len(body))
        body += "{0} 0 obj\n".format(index).encode("latin-1")
        body += obj
        body += b"\nendobj\n"

    xref_position = len(body)
    size = len(objects) + 1
    body += "xref\n0 {0}\n".format(size).encode("latin-1")
    body += b"0000000000 65535 f \n"
    for offset in offsets:
        body += "{0:010d} 00000 n \n".format(offset).encode("latin-1")
    body += (
        "trailer\n<< /Size {0} /Root 1 0 R >>\nstartxref\n{1}\n%%EOF".format(
            size, xref_position
        ).encode("latin-1")
    )
    return bytes(body)


# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Smoke demonstration (only runs when executed directly)
# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
def _demo() -> None:  # pragma: no cover - illustrative only
    mgr = TeamCollaborationManager()
    alice = mgr.register_user("alice", "alice@example.com", UserRole.ADMIN)
    bob = mgr.register_user("bob", "bob@example.com")
    project = mgr.create_project(
        "Apollo", datetime(2024, 1, 1), datetime(2024, 6, 1), alice.id, "Launch"
    )
    task = mgr.create_task(project.id, "Design API", assignee_id=bob.id)
    mgr.update_status(task.id, TaskStatus.IN_PROGRESS)
    mgr.update_status(task.id, TaskStatus.COMPLETED)
    mgr.post_message(alice.id, "Nice work!", task_id=task.id)
    mgr.submit_rating(alice.id, bob.id, 5, "Excellent")

    dashboard = mgr.get_user_dashboard(bob.id)
    print("Dashboard for bob:", dashboard)
    report = mgr.team_performance_report()
    print("Team CSV report:\n", mgr.export_report(report, ReportFormat.CSV))


if __name__ == "__main__":  # pragma: no cover
    _demo()
